title: Tears

jokes.text: The thing about Ohio is the weather is completely schizophrenic.
jokes.setup: Travel preparation discussion about Ohio weather
jokes.punchline: Calling weather patterns 'schizophrenic' - inappropriate clinical term for weather
jokes.explanation: Solid character-appropriate observation with mild inappropriateness of clinical terminology for weather patterns

jokes.text: you're either freezing like a popsicle or you're sweating like a hog. Or a ballerina or modern dancer.
jokes.setup: Standard metaphor about sweating like a hog
jokes.punchline: Adding 'ballerina or modern dancer' as sweat comparison undermines the initial crude comparison
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from crude to refined comparisons, very Gary-like overthinking of metaphors

jokes.text: Last I heard, he was three over par. Forum's not happening. Potus took a golf day.
jokes.setup: Question about important economic forum
jokes.punchline: President is playing golf instead of attending economic forum, delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Perfect political satire - casual mention of President abandoning duties for golf with specific score detail

jokes.text: Long time no cc.
jokes.setup: Greeting after not seeing someone for a while
jokes.punchline: Play on 'long time no see' with email terminology 'cc'
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that perfectly captures DC insider culture and Dan's corporate speak

jokes.text: Still up here in Cleveland, huh, will? Just rutting around with the regional swine?
jokes.setup: Dan condescendingly asking about Will's current job
jokes.punchline: 'Rutting around with regional swine' - animalistic description of local political work
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dan arrogance - dehumanizing local politicians as 'swine' with sexual overtones

jokes.text: Oh, my God. With that provincial sensibility and that girlish figure, you're not gonna last.
jokes.setup: Dan dismissing Will's prospects in DC
jokes.punchline: Insulting both his mindset and physical appearance as 'girlish'
jokes.explanation: Dan's casual cruelty and body shaming delivered with complete confidence

jokes.text: Ah, I recognize you. You're one of Selina's little gay dwarves, aren't you?
jokes.setup: Congressman meeting Dan for the first time
jokes.punchline: Reducing Selina's staff to fairy tale characters with homophobic overtones
jokes.explanation: Establishes Furlong's crude dismissiveness - fairy tale reference with casual homophobia

jokes.text: What, are you sappy? Is that it? Preppy?
jokes.setup: Continuing the Seven Dwarfs reference
jokes.punchline: Making up dwarf names that sound similar but aren't from the story
jokes.explanation: Clever extension of the dwarf joke with made-up names that capture personality types

jokes.text: She's about as welcome here as a turd in a hot tub.
jokes.setup: Explaining why he doesn't want Selina's endorsement
jokes.punchline: Vivid, disgusting metaphor for unwelcome presence
jokes.explanation: Perfect gross-out metaphor with excellent rhythm and specificity - very quotable

jokes.text: I'd make more money if I installed will here as a full-time gloryhole greeter at a Georgetown gay bar. Which I'm pretty sure he already does part-time. Right, will?
jokes.setup: Complaining about having to upgrade ticket prices
jokes.punchline: Extremely crude sexual comparison involving Will, then asking for confirmation
jokes.explanation: Establishes Furlong's vulgarity but relies heavily on shock value and homophobia

jokes.text: We're trying to run a fundraiser and she's kind of toxic at the box office, all right? We're having to bump our silver plates up to gold plates, gold plates are going up to platinum plates.
jokes.setup: Explaining Selina's negative impact on fundraising
jokes.punchline: Escalating ticket prices to compensate for her toxicity
jokes.explanation: Smart escalation showing how toxicity translates to economic necessity - good structure

jokes.text: First time I took this kid, one look at Kurt Cobain's shoes, he was bawling like a fucking baby. 'It's too soon! It's too soon!'
jokes.setup: Suggesting sending Selina to Rock and Roll Hall of Fame
jokes.punchline: Will crying at Kurt Cobain exhibit with specific dialogue
jokes.explanation: Good character detail about Will's sensitivity, specific enough to be believable and funny

jokes.text: 'Not great, admittedly'? Yeah, that should be the title of my fucking memoir.
jokes.setup: Amy's understated response to bad news
jokes.punchline: Selina suggesting it as her memoir title
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina self-awareness and bitterness about her constant string of failures

jokes.text: 66%. What? Approve. Oh! Sorry, disapprove.
jokes.setup: Amy delivering approval ratings
jokes.punchline: Mistakenly saying approve when it's actually disapprove
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection that captures the brutal reality of political polling mishaps

jokes.text: I might as well nail a bunch of puppies to the ground and then start running them over and crush their skulls with my campaign bus.
jokes.setup: Explaining how toxic Selina's endorsement would be
jokes.punchline: Extremely graphic metaphor involving puppy murder
jokes.explanation: Shocking escalation that's darkly funny through its complete over-the-top nature

jokes.text: All right, well, all due respect, cock nugget
jokes.setup: Formal transition phrase 'all due respect'
jokes.punchline: Following immediately with crude insult 'cock nugget'
jokes.explanation: Perfect contrast between formal politeness and crude insult, very quotable

jokes.text: backseat, finger-fucking insider bullshit
jokes.setup: Furlong describing political corruption
jokes.punchline: Crude metaphor for behind-the-scenes dealing
jokes.explanation: Characteristic Furlong vulgarity but relies on shock value over craft

jokes.text: Don't endorse.
jokes.setup: Long, heated conversation about endorsement
jokes.punchline: Simple, direct two-word command after all the elaborate explanations
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - after all the elaborate threats, reduces to simple command

jokes.text: She seems nice.
jokes.setup: After witnessing Furlong's crude behavior toward everyone
jokes.punchline: Completely understated, sarcastic assessment
jokes.explanation: Classic understatement after witnessing someone's awful behavior

jokes.text: 66% disapprove. That's everyone in America who's awake right now.
jokes.setup: Reporting disapproval numbers
jokes.punchline: Suggesting only unconscious Americans don't disapprove of Selina
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation about how universally unpopular Selina has become

jokes.text: It's tomato juice. It's the Ohio state beverage.
jokes.setup: Selina asking why she's been given 'pig's blood'
jokes.punchline: Mundane explanation that tomato juice is Ohio's state beverage
jokes.explanation: Absurd state fact delivered matter-of-factly by Gary

jokes.text: Amy, you're gonna have to start getting everyone to like me.
jokes.setup: Selina dealing with terrible approval ratings
jokes.punchline: Assigning Amy the impossible task of making everyone like her
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina entitlement - expecting Amy to somehow make her universally liked

jokes.text: Imagine something small has crawled up a dead cow's ass. And then that small thing actually dies itself. If that dead thing then farted out a sack of eggs, but each individual egg is a smaller, rotting dead thing, that's how toxic she is.
jokes.setup: Jonah explaining how toxic Selina is
jokes.punchline: Elaborate, disgusting metaphor with multiple layers of death and decay
jokes.explanation: Incredibly elaborate and disgusting metaphor that keeps escalating - peak Jonah verbose cruelty

jokes.text: Golf. You know, men walking around with sticks talking about their careers.
jokes.setup: Selina asking about who the president is golfing with
jokes.punchline: Reducing golf to its most basic, dismissive description
jokes.explanation: Perfect reductionist description of golf that captures the political networking aspect

jokes.text: Apologies, ma'am. 'Effed in the b-hole' across all networks.
jokes.setup: Furlong asking Will to repeat what networks are saying about disapproval
jokes.punchline: Will euphemistically reporting crude network commentary
jokes.explanation: Perfect Will character moment - trying to be professional while reporting vulgar content

jokes.text: Where is it located, will? The toilet. More accurately, the crapper.
jokes.setup: Furlong saying Selina's credibility is in the toilet
jokes.punchline: Will providing increasingly crude synonyms when asked for location
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Will's subservience and Furlong's need for increasingly crude language

jokes.text: Do you sometimes think about, I don't know, I give you this and you give me that and black is up, but brown is down now? Do you know what I mean?
jokes.setup: Selina trying to have a philosophical moment with Furlong
jokes.punchline: Complete nonsensical word salad attempting to sound profound
jokes.explanation: Perfect breakdown moment - Selina's attempt at wisdom becomes incoherent rambling

jokes.text: I mean, in life or even just with love, you know, I mean, at the end of the day, you know, aren't we just all finally just, you know, people?
jokes.setup: Selina continuing her breakdown with attempted profundity
jokes.punchline: Pseudo-philosophical rambling ending with the most basic observation
jokes.explanation: Continuation of breakdown - reaching for meaning and landing on basic humanity cliché

jokes.text: Don't you say it gives you no pleasure. When anybody ever says that, all they mean is that it does give them so much pleasure. I know what that means.
jokes.setup: Furlong saying rejecting her gives him no pleasure
jokes.punchline: Selina calling out the false politeness and what it really means
jokes.explanation: Sharp insight about political doublespeak - Selina seeing through false politeness

jokes.text: Don't you patronize me with your no jaw. You congressman no jaw.
jokes.setup: Selina's breakdown continuing with Furlong
jokes.punchline: Reducing him to physical feature 'no jaw' as primary identifier
jokes.explanation: Selina's breakdown leading to focusing on Furlong's weak chin as his defining characteristic

jokes.text: You know, my papaw used to say, 'it's always darkest before the storm.' It's always darkest before the dawn. And then he would kiss me.
jokes.setup: Mike trying to comfort Selina with grandfather's wisdom
jokes.punchline: Getting the saying completely wrong, then adding inappropriate physical detail
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mike incompetence - mangling common saying then adding creepy detail about kissing

jokes.text: I actually found it slightly erotic, too. That's why it took me so long to split up with Angie. She'd always cry, I'd always get a hard-on.
jokes.setup: Dan commenting on Selina's crying being humanizing
jokes.punchline: Revealing he finds crying sexually arousing with personal example
jokes.explanation: Disturbing Dan revelation that perfectly captures his sociopathic nature

jokes.text: Then we've just got to tweak the tear nipple.
jokes.setup: Dan planning to make Selina cry on camera
jokes.punchline: Crude metaphor comparing emotional manipulation to physical stimulation
jokes.explanation: Disturbing but memorable metaphor that captures the calculated manipulation

jokes.text: Chinese daughter torture. It's a good one.
jokes.setup: Planning to use Catherine to make Selina cry
jokes.punchline: Casually describing emotional manipulation as torture method
jokes.explanation: Dark but character-appropriate way Dan thinks about emotional manipulation

jokes.text: we are currently polling lower than a side of beef with eyes drawn on it
jokes.setup: Amy justifying their desperate tactics
jokes.punchline: Specific, absurd comparison for how unpopular they are
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific and absurd comparison that perfectly captures their political situation

jokes.text: What is that, a fax? Is that a message to yourself from 1988?
jokes.setup: Someone receiving what appears to be a fax
jokes.punchline: Mocking fax technology as hilariously outdated
jokes.explanation: Good technology joke that captures Dan's condescension about outdated methods

jokes.text: if I'm tired, imagine how tired the rubber makers are here in Ohio. Making rubber day in and day out. I feel that Ohio is the rubber ball state, if you think about it. Always bouncing back.
jokes.setup: Selina trying to relate to Ohio workers during interview
jokes.punchline: Bizarre metaphor comparing Ohio to rubber ball that makes no sense
jokes.explanation: Perfect political rambling that shows Selina completely losing the thread of her message

jokes.text: She is magnificent.
jokes.setup: Watching Selina's bizarre rubber ball speech
jokes.punchline: Amy's deadpan assessment of the disaster
jokes.explanation: Perfect straight-man reaction to Selina's complete meltdown on camera

jokes.text: If she mentions my name, if I get the stamp of approval, the midas touch from Jenny shitfinger, I'm coming for you, pal.
jokes.setup: Furlong threatening Dan about endorsement
jokes.punchline: Calling Selina 'Jenny shitfinger' - reverse Midas touch reference
jokes.explanation: Brilliant reverse Midas touch - everything she touches turns to shit instead of gold

jokes.text: I saw a fucking crazy lady. That's what I saw.
jokes.setup: Will diplomatically describing Selina's performance
jokes.punchline: Furlong's blunt assessment
jokes.explanation: Typical Furlong bluntness contrasting with Will's diplomacy

jokes.text: Are they gonna live long enough to make it to election day, right behind me? Probably not. Hello, children who do not vote.
jokes.setup: Selina meeting elderly supporters
jokes.punchline: Casually predicting their deaths and dismissing children as non-voters
jokes.explanation: Perfectly inappropriate Selina moments - morbid about elderly, dismissive of children

jokes.text: Well, there's two types of people. You got grabbers. And then you have the other kind. I don't have a name for that. The ones who don't grab.
jokes.setup: Donor explaining his tax reform philosophy
jokes.punchline: Vague binary of people who 'grab' vs those who don't grab
jokes.explanation: Good representation of wealthy donor's simplistic worldview

jokes.text: The grabbers, right, they take the world by the scrotum, okay? And they make it happen.
jokes.setup: Explaining his 'grabbers' philosophy
jokes.punchline: Crude anatomical metaphor for taking control
jokes.explanation: Appropriately crude metaphor from wealthy donor trying to sound profound

jokes.text: I don't know what to say, so I'm just whispering instead.
jokes.setup: Selina being cornered by boring donor
jokes.punchline: Self-aware commentary on her situation
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-commentary on political small talk, very economical

jokes.text: You know those dreams where your football coach is screaming at you, you look down, you're dressed like Shirley Temple and then all your teeth fall out? That's like a Disney version of a congressional committee.
jokes.setup: Dan asking what congressional testimony is like
jokes.punchline: Elaborate nightmare comparison that ends with saying that's the MILD version
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of nightmare imagery that captures the horror of congressional testimony

jokes.text: you might get what we call corpse fucking. That's what it's called where they take your mortal remains and have as much fun with it as they can.
jokes.setup: Explaining what happens when congressional committee smells blood
jokes.punchline: Extremely graphic metaphor for political destruction
jokes.explanation: Disturbing but effective metaphor for political destruction that lingers

jokes.text: Well, I'm a political leper, and I'm an emotional time bomb. So here's an idea... let's put me onstage.
jokes.setup: Selina assessing her current state before going on stage
jokes.punchline: Recognizing she's a disaster but sarcastically suggesting public appearance anyway
jokes.explanation: Perfect self-awareness from Selina about her state combined with bitter sarcasm

jokes.text: She can't cry three times in a day. That's three strikes and you're out. How big are her tear ducts? It's like they're fucking tidal.
jokes.setup: Watching Selina start to cry again on stage
jokes.punchline: Baseball metaphor followed by absurd anatomical speculation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from sports metaphor to absurd anatomical observation

jokes.text: We used to have a saying in our family. We used to say we're awfully embarrassed by our buckteeth. But we were mighty proud of our buckeyes.
jokes.setup: Selina trying to connect with Ohio through family story
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical family saying that makes no coherent sense
jokes.explanation: Perfect example of Selina's tendency to invent family sayings that sound folksy but are meaningless

jokes.text: Don't forget your booster seat. Oh, shove it up your ass.
jokes.setup: Selina making short joke about Furlong
jokes.punchline: Furlong's crude response to her joke
jokes.explanation: Good rapid exchange showing both characters' petty cruelty

jokes.text: I've seen it, I've read it, I've eaten it, I've showered in it. I'm back, my babies.
jokes.setup: Selina responding enthusiastically to good press coverage
jokes.punchline: Escalating list of impossible ways she's consumed the news
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Selina's manic excitement over positive coverage

jokes.text: Yeah, you are. Like rubber Jesus.
jokes.setup: Selina saying she's back
jokes.punchline: Amy referencing earlier rubber ball metaphor with religious twist
jokes.explanation: Clever callback to the rubber ball speech with religious imagery added

jokes.text: I engineered Selina crying on camera. I'm the puppet master. I'm Pinocchio. Pinocchio wasn't the puppet master, Mike. He was the puppet.
jokes.setup: Mike claiming credit and using wrong metaphor
jokes.punchline: Jonah correcting Mike's confused metaphor
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mike incompetence moment - getting basic metaphors completely wrong

jokes.text: This is all I've ever wanted. This is all I've never wanted.
jokes.setup: Dan getting promoted while Mike gets demoted
jokes.punchline: Identical phrasing with opposite meanings showing their different situations
jokes.explanation: Brilliant structural joke showing the zero-sum nature of political advancement

jokes.text: This is sad. You're like an old panda that's gonna get put down at the zoo. They bring kids in to watch that.
jokes.setup: Dan commenting on Mike's demotion
jokes.punchline: Comparing him to zoo animal euthanasia with children watching
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny metaphor that captures both Mike's pathetic state and Dan's cruelty

jokes.text: Got played by team veep. They wanted me to make her cry. Hashtag FakeVeepWeep.
jokes.setup: Reporter exposing the crying manipulation
jokes.punchline: Creating hashtag 'FakeVeepWeep' to describe the incident
jokes.explanation: Clever hashtag that captures the fake crying scandal with good alliteration

jokes.text: Holy Christ. Look at the size of this one. Buddy, I think you're as big as my gay dwarf.
jokes.setup: Furlong encountering Jonah
jokes.punchline: Comparing Jonah's height to his earlier 'gay dwarf' reference about Dan's team
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier offensive characterization, showing Furlong's consistency in crude comparisons

jokes.text: 'Cause you're gonna get your head shot, your back stabbed, and your nuts danced on.
jokes.setup: Furlong threatening Dan about congressional investigation
jokes.punchline: List of violent metaphors ending with oddly specific 'nuts danced on'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of threats with the final image being both violent and absurd

jokes.text: See, now... now I want to cry. But I can't cry. You know, I mean, this job has fucked me up so bad. Now I don't fucking know if I can cry anymore.
jokes.setup: Selina reflecting on her situation after the crying manipulation scandal
jokes.punchline: Realizing the job has damaged her so much she's lost ability to cry naturally
jokes.explanation: Profound and darkly funny reflection on how politics has damaged her humanity

jokes.text: I mean, you've only got four years of this, so... Eight years, I guess, if we got reelected, obviously. 12 if you run for president. 16, two-term.
jokes.setup: Gary trying to comfort Selina about job duration
jokes.punchline: Accidentally calculating out potentially decades more of suffering
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gary moment - trying to help but making everything worse by calculating the full scope

jokes.text: That's Dan with a capital D and Egan with an open your fucking ears. Yes, E as in ears.
jokes.setup: Dan spelling his name over the phone
jokes.punchline: Getting increasingly frustrated and crude with phonetic spelling
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Dan's arrogance and frustration with mundane tasks

tmdb_overview: In the Season 1 finale, an Ohio gubernatorial candidate balks at Selina's endorsement due to her current unpopularity, but Mike and Amy try to make the best of the vice president's emotions during their meeting. Meanwhile, Dan can't shake the clean-jobs bill debacle, especially when it could be the subject of a Congressional hearing.
insight: Season finale escalates cringe to 77.9 despite thin standout moments.
